summary: Do not ship: the RED→GREEN lock is intact, but the shared PR-observation seam treats conflicting ref facts as authoritative absence, reopening the destructive adopt/create path and a silent revocation gap.

findings.title: Blocker: ref absence and an empty PR connection collapse into the same state
findings.body: `foldConnection` maps `repository.ref === null` directly to an empty `PrScan`, losing the distinction between “ref absent” and “ref present with a completely enumerated empty PR connection.” The main path later observes the ref independently with `git ls-remote` and selects `adopt` whenever that second read finds it (lines 1510-1513 and 1698-1700). Reconciliation is worse: it starts with refs returned by `ls-remote`, yet a subsequent `ref:null` becomes `pr:null` and exits successfully (lines 1183-1196 and 1405-1417). Because these reads are non-atomic, ref recreation or a stale/degraded GraphQL view can hide an existing PR. Commit ownership proves only the head shape, not PR absence, so the executor may force-push and attempt another PR creation, while reconciliation may leave an armed PR untouched without turning the run red. W75 at `tools/tests/test_ensure-bump-pr.bats:1228-1238` explicitly locks the contradictory `ref:null` plus branch-present state as successful adoption.
findings.recommendation: Represent the read as a discriminated ref observation including presence and target OID, then cross-check it against `ls-remote`. Permit create only when both report absence; permit adopt only when GraphQL reports a present ref with a fully enumerated empty connection and matching OID. Treat disagreement as zero-mutation failure, and as `revocationBlind` in reconciliation. Strengthen W75 accordingly, add the reconciliation mismatch witness, retain W74/W3, and rebuild the fixed-test RED/GREEN pins.

next_steps: Fix the ref-observation seam before building further on the executor.
Regenerate machine-owned RED/GREEN records with the strengthened tests and unchanged four-path production scope.
